data.content:
### Disable Dexie Cloud Data Sync Service Worker

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/dexie-cloud-todo-app/README.md

To prevent Dexie Cloud from using its service worker for data synchronization, you need to modify the db.cloud.configure() call in db.ts. Specifically, remove or comment out the tryUseServiceWorker: true configuration option from this method.

```TypeScript
tryUseServiceWorker: true
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Dexie Cloud ToDo App Database Connection

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/dexie-cloud-todo-app/README.md

This script configures the application to connect to your Dexie Cloud database. It automatically creates a .env.local file and sets the REACT_APP_DBURL environment variable based on the database URL found in dexie-cloud.json.

```Shell
./configure-app.sh
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Dexie Cloud ToDo App and Whitelist for PWA

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/dexie-cloud-todo-app/README.md

These commands facilitate the deployment of the Dexie Cloud ToDo application to GitHub Pages, making it accessible as a Progressive Web App (PWA). The npx dexie-cloud whitelist command is crucial for enabling service worker functionality on the deployed URL, allowing for offline capabilities and periodic syncing.

```Shell
npm run deploy
npx dexie-cloud whitelist https://your-github-username.github.io
```

--------------------------------

### Set Up Dexie Cloud ToDo App Locally

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/dexie-cloud-todo-app/README.md

This sequence of commands sets up the Dexie Cloud ToDo application for local development. It includes creating a new database in the cloud, installing project dependencies, configuring the app to connect to the database, and finally starting the development server.

```Shell
npx dexie-cloud create
npm install
./configure-app.sh
npm start
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Dexie Database with Cloud Addon in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/dexie-cloud/README.md

This TypeScript code demonstrates the basic setup for a Dexie database integrated with the Dexie Cloud addon. It includes importing necessary modules, instantiating Dexie, defining a database schema, and configuring the cloud database URL.

```typescript
import Dexie from 'dexie';
import dexieCloud from 'dexie-cloud-addon';

const db = new Dexie('dbname', { addons: [dexieCloud]});

db.version(1).stores({
    yourTable: '@primKeyProp, indexedProp1, indexedProp2, ...'
});

db.cloud.configure({
  databaseUrl: 'https://<yourdb>.dexie.cloud'
})
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dexie and Dexie Cloud Addon via npm

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/dexie-cloud/README.md

This snippet shows how to install the core Dexie library and the Dexie Cloud addon using npm. These packages are essential for integrating Dexie Cloud functionalities into your web application.

```shell
npm install dexie@latest
npm install dexie-cloud-addon@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dexie Syncable and Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Syncable/README.md

Instructions to install Dexie, Dexie Observable, and Dexie Syncable packages using npm, which are required for two-way synchronization.

```shell
npm install dexie --save
npm install dexie-observable --save
npm install dexie-syncable --save
```

--------------------------------

### Include Dexie Syncable Libraries in HTML

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Syncable/README.md

Example of including Dexie, Dexie Observable, Dexie Syncable, and an example sync protocol implementation via script tags in an HTML file for browser-based usage.

```html
<html><head>
        <script src="dexie.min.js"></script>
        <script src="dexie-observable.min.js"></script>
        <script src="dexie-syncable.min.js"></script>
        <script src="WebSocketSyncProtocol.js"></script> <!-- Example implementation of ISyncProtocol -->
        ...
    </head><body>
    </body></html>
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Dexie Cloud Database URL using CLI

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/dexie-cloud/README.md

This command-line snippet shows how to use the Dexie Cloud CLI to generate a unique database URL. This URL is crucial for connecting your Dexie application to a Dexie Cloud backend.

```shell
npx dexie-cloud create
```

--------------------------------

### Connect Dexie Syncable to a Remote Server

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Syncable/README.md

Example of connecting the Dexie Syncable instance to a remote synchronization server using a specified protocol (e.g., 'websocket') and URL. It also demonstrates how to listen for and log status changes of the synchronization connection.

```javascript
// This example uses the WebSocketSyncProtocol included in earlier steps.
db.syncable.connect ("websocket", "https://syncserver.com/sync");
db.syncable.on('statusChanged', function (newStatus, url) {
    console.log ("Sync Status changed: " + Dexie.Syncable.StatusTexts[newStatus]);
});
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Dexie Cloud ToDo App to GitHub Pages

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/dexie-cloud-todo-app/README.md

This command deploys the production-ready build of the Dexie Cloud ToDo application to the gh-pages branch of the current GitHub repository. The application will then be accessible under the /dexie-cloud-todo-app/ path on your GitHub Pages site.

```Shell
npm run deploy
```

--------------------------------

### Build Dexie Cloud ToDo App for Production

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/dexie-cloud-todo-app/README.md

This command builds the Dexie Cloud ToDo application for production deployment. It optimizes the React application, minifies code, and bundles all necessary assets into the build folder, preparing it for optimal performance in a production environment.

```Shell
npm run build
```

--------------------------------

### Start Dexie Cloud ToDo App Development Server

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/dexie-cloud-todo-app/README.md

This command runs the Dexie Cloud ToDo application in development mode. It starts a local server, typically accessible at http://localhost:3000, and enables features like hot module reloading for a smooth development experience.

```Shell
npm start
```

--------------------------------

### Perform Database Operations with Dexie Syncable

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Syncable/README.md

Demonstrates performing read and write operations within a Dexie transaction. Any changes made through these operations will be automatically replicated to the server and other connected clients by Dexie Syncable.

```javascript
db.transaction('rw', db.friends, function (friends) {
    friends.add({name: "Arne", shoeSize: 47});
    friends.where('shoeSize').above(40).each(function (friend) {
        console.log("Friend with shoeSize over 40: " + friend.name);
    });
});
```

--------------------------------

### Import Dexie Syncable in JavaScript

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Syncable/README.md

Demonstrates how to import Dexie and Dexie Syncable into a JavaScript application. Importing 'dexie-syncable' will also implicitly import 'dexie-observable'.

```javascript
import Dexie from 'dexie';
import 'dexie-syncable'; // will import dexie-observable as well.
```

--------------------------------

### Disable Application Resource Caching Service Worker

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/dexie-cloud-todo-app/README.md

To disable the application's service worker responsible for caching resources, modify the index.tsx file. Change the call from serviceWorkerRegistration.register(); to serviceWorkerRegistration.unregister(); to prevent the service worker from registering and caching application assets.

```TypeScript
// Change this:
serviceWorkerRegistration.register();
// To this:
serviceWorkerRegistration.unregister();
```

--------------------------------

### Run Dexie Cloud ToDo App Tests

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/dexie-cloud-todo-app/README.md

Executes the test runner for the Dexie Cloud ToDo application. This command launches an interactive watch mode, allowing developers to continuously monitor test results as code changes are made, ensuring code quality and functionality.

```Shell
npm test
```

--------------------------------

### Upgrade Existing Dexie Database for Syncable

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Syncable/README.md

Shows how to upgrade an existing Dexie database schema to enable Dexie Syncable. This involves adding a new version to trigger the addon's installation of its internal tables without altering existing user tables.

```javascript
import Dexie from 'dexie';
import 'dexie-observable';
import 'dexie-syncable';
import 'your-sync-protocol-implementation';

var db = new Dexie('myExistingDb');
db.version(1).stores(... existing schema ...);

// Now, add another version, just to trigger an upgrade for Dexie.Syncable
db.version(2).stores({}); // No need to add / remove tables. This is just to allow the addon to install its tables.
```

--------------------------------

### Define Dexie Syncable Schema with UUID Primary Keys

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Syncable/README.md

Illustrates how to define a Dexie database schema using the `$$` prefix for primary keys. This ensures universally unique identifiers (UUIDs) are generated, which are essential for two-way replication across multiple sync nodes.

```javascript
var db = new Dexie("MySyncedDB");
db.version(1).stores({
    friends: "$$oid,name,shoeSize",
    pets: "$$oid,name,kind"
});
```

--------------------------------

### Demonstrate Dexie liveQuery with CRUD operations

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/samples/liveQuery/liveQuery.html

This JavaScript code initializes a Dexie database, defines a 'friends' store, and uses `liveQuery` to observe changes to friends within a specific age range. It then performs a series of asynchronous CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations on the database, logging the results of the live query to the console. It showcases how `liveQuery` automatically reacts to database modifications.

```JavaScript
{ "imports": { "dexie": "https://unpkg.com/dexie@^3.2/dist/modern/dexie.min.mjs" } } import Dexie, { liveQuery } from 'dexie'; const db = new Dexie('MyDatabase'); db.version(1).stores({ friends: '++id, name, age', }); const friendsObservable = liveQuery(() => db.friends.where('age').between(50, 75).toArray() ); const subscription = friendsObservable.subscribe({ next: (result) => console.log('Got result:', JSON.stringify(result)), error: (error) => console.error(error), }); const sleep = (ms) => new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms)); await sleep(1000); console.log('1. Adding friend'); const friendId = await db.friends.add({ name: 'Magdalena', age: 54 }); await sleep(1000); console.log('2. Changing age to 99'); await db.friends.update(friendId, { age: 99 }); await sleep(1000); console.log('3. Changing age to 55'); await db.friends.update(friendId, { age: 55 }); await sleep(1000); console.log("4. Setting property 'foo' to 'bar'"); await db.friends.update(friendId, { foo: 'bar' }); await sleep(1000); console.log('5. Deleting friend'); await db.friends.delete(friendId); subscription.unsubscribe();
```

--------------------------------

### Configure WebWorker Test Environment for Dexie Addons

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Syncable/test/integration/test-syncable-dexie-tests.html

This JavaScript snippet sets up global variables `window.workerSource` and `window.workerImports` to configure a WebWorker test environment. It specifies the main worker script and lists the Dexie core library along with its Observable and Syncable addons to be imported within the worker.

```JavaScript
window.workerSource = "worker.js";
window.workerImports = [ "../dist/dexie.js", "../addons/Dexie.Observable/dist/dexie-observable.js", "../addons/Dexie.Syncable/dist/dexie-syncable.js" ];
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dexie via Bower and List Dexie.Syncable Distribution Files

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Syncable/dist/README.md

This snippet first installs the main Dexie.js library using Bower, which, for versions 1.3.4 and above, includes its addons like Dexie.Syncable. The second command then lists the contents of the dist directory for the Dexie.Syncable addon within the bower_components folder, showing the available distribution files.

```shell
$ bower install dexie --save
$ ls bower_components/dexie/addons/Dexie.Syncable/dist
```

--------------------------------

### Upgrade Existing Dexie Database for Observable

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Observable/README.md

Shows how to upgrade an existing Dexie database schema to properly integrate Dexie.Observable. A new version increment is necessary to trigger the add-on's installation of its internal tables, even if no user-defined tables are modified.

```JavaScript
import Dexie from 'dexie';
import 'dexie-observable';

var db = new Dexie('myExistingDb');
db.version(1).stores(... existing schema ...);

// Now, add another version, just to trigger an upgrade for Dexie.Observable
db.version(2).stores({}); // No need to add / remove tables. This is just to allow the addon to install its tables.
```

--------------------------------

### Import Dexie and Dexie.Observable

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/Dexie.Observable/README.md

Demonstrates how to import the Dexie core library and the Dexie.Observable add-on into a JavaScript project. This setup allows for normal Dexie database operations while also enabling the new observable capabilities.

```JavaScript
import Dexie from 'dexie';
import 'dexie-observable';

// Use Dexie as normally - but you can also subscribe to db.on('changes').
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Usage of Dexie Export/Import

Source: https://github.com/dexie/dexie.js/blob/master/addons/dexie-export-import/README.md

Demonstrates the fundamental ways to use the dexie-export-import module. It shows how to import a database from a Blob to a new Dexie instance, export an existing database to a Blob, and import data from a Blob into an already initialized Dexie instance.

```javascript
import Dexie from "dexie";
import "dexie-export-import";

//
// Import from Blob or File to Dexie instance:
//
const db = await Dexie.import(blob, [options]);

//
// Export to Blob
//
const blob = await db.export([options]);

//
// Import from Blob or File to existing Dexie instance
//
await db.import(blob, [options]);
```
